terms.definition: Degree of Freedom. Independent displacement/rotation at a node. Solid node: 3 translational. Shell node: 5-6.
Stress that increases without bound under mesh refinement. Caused by sharp corners, point loads, point constraints. Not physical.
Global [K] from assembled element matrices. [K]{u}={F} gives displacements. Singular if rigid body motion possible.
Geometric nonlinearity in ANSYS. Enables strain on deformed geometry. Required when δ/L > 5%.
Open (no load transfer) or Closed (contact pressure active). Status changes = contact nonlinearity.
10-node quadratic tetrahedron (SOLID187). Midside nodes; accurate for bending and curved boundaries. ANSYS default for CAD geometry.
Equivalent stress for ductile failure: σ_v = √[((σ1-σ2)²+(σ2-σ3)²+(σ3-σ1)²)/2]. ANSYS reports as Equivalent Stress.
Factor of Safety = σ_yield / σ_vonMises. FOS < 1: yielding predicted. FOS = 1: at limit. FOS = 2: 50% margin.
Artificial stiffness in linear elements under bending. Resolved by higher-order elements (TET10, HEXA20) or reduced integration.
N_i(x): interpolates displacement between nodes. Linear elements: straight; quadratic: parabolic interpolation.
Strain-displacement matrix: {ε}=[B]{u}^e. Derivative of shape functions. Used in element stiffness: [k]=∫[B]ᵀ[D][B]dV.
